meanings.usage: 形容空间狭小而拥挤，通常指房间、车厢等地方
形容空气不流通、令人感到闷热的环境
形容缺乏变化、令人感到无聊的事物
指通过有节奏的声音或动作使人放松、想要入睡
特指火车上的卧铺座位或卧铺车厢
指在黑暗中或不方便的情况下笨拙地寻找东西
修饰动词，表示某事必然会发生
英国英语，指专供机动车辆高速行驶的公路
指运送人、车等穿过河流、海峡等水域的船
指通过威胁或气势使人感到害怕或胆怯

meanings.meaning: 令人兴奋的；使人高兴的
meanings.usage: 形容令人感到非常兴奋和愉悦的体验
指鸟或飞机在高空中飞翔，也可用于抽象意义
形容景色或事物极其壮观、美得令人屏息

meanings.meaning: 逃避现实者；追求逃避的人
meanings.usage: 指喜欢逃避现实、追求消遣娱乐的人
指人到达目的地后依然精神焕发、精力充沛

phrases.usage: 转移注意力；使不再想某事

examples.en: Reading is only a partial solution, for the monotonous rhythm of the wheels clicking on the rails soon lull you to sleep.
examples.zh: 阅读只是部分解决办法，因为车轮撞击铁轨的单调节奏很快就会让你昏昏欲睡。

examples.en: He tried to take his mind off the problem by watching TV.
examples.zh: 他试图通过看电视来转移注意力，不再想那个问题。

phrases.usage: 断断续续地；时有时无地

examples.en: During the day, sleep comes in snatches.
examples.zh: 白天的时候，睡眠断断续续。

examples.en: We only got the information in snatches.
examples.zh: 我们只是断断续续地得到这些信息。

examples.en: By comparison, ferry trips or cruises offer a great variety of civilized comforts.
examples.zh: 相比之下，渡船旅行或游轮提供了多种多样的文明享受。

examples.en: By comparison, city life is more exciting.
examples.zh: 相比之下，城市生活更加精彩。

examples.en: You can stretch your legs on the spacious decks, play games, meet interesting people and enjoy good food.
examples.zh: 你可以在宽敞的甲板上伸展双腿、做游戏、结识有趣的人、享用美食。

examples.en: We've been driving for hours. Let's stop and stretch our legs.
examples.zh: 我们已经开了几个小时的车了，停下来活动一下吧。

examples.en: Aeroplanes have the reputation of being dangerous and even hardened travellers are intimidated by them.
examples.zh: 飞机有危险的名声，甚至老练的旅行者也对其感到畏惧。

examples.en: She has the reputation of being very reliable.
examples.zh: 她有非常可靠的名声。

examples.en: For a few hours, you settle back in a deep armchair to enjoy the flight.
examples.zh: 在几个小时内，你可以靠在扶手椅上享受飞行。

examples.en: Settle back and enjoy the show.
examples.zh: 坐好享受演出吧。

examples.en: The journey is so smooth that there is nothing to prevent you from reading or sleeping.
examples.zh: 旅途如此平稳，没有什么能阻止你阅读或睡觉。

examples.en: Nothing can prevent me from achieving my goals.
examples.zh: 没有什么能阻止我实现目标。

examples.en: but more often than not, the greater part of the journey is spent on roads with few service stations and too much traffic.
examples.zh: 但更多的时候，大部分旅程是在只有很少服务站和交通拥挤的道路上度过的。

examples.en: More often than not, she arrives late for meetings.
examples.zh: 她开会经常迟到。

examples.en: enjoy good food--always assuming, of course, that the sea is calm.
examples.zh: 享用美食——当然，前提是海面平静。

examples.en: I'll help you, always assuming that you need my assistance.
examples.zh: 我会帮你的，只要你需要我的帮助。

examples.en: An aeroplane gets you to your destination rapidly.
examples.zh: 飞机会快速把你送到目的地。

examples.en: The bus will get you to the station on time.
examples.zh: 公共汽车会把你准时送到车站。

grammar.title: 否定副词开头的倒装句 (Inversion with Negative Adverbs)
grammar.definition: 当否定副词或否定意义的词组位于句首时，句子需要使用部分倒装语序，即把助动词或be动词提到主语前面。
grammar.structure: 否定词 + 助动词/be动词 + 主语 + 动词原形...
grammar.usage: 用于强调否定意义，常见的否定词包括：hardly, never, rarely, seldom, not until, no sooner, little等。

examples.en: Hardly anyone can positively enjoy sitting in a train for more than a few hours.
examples.zh: 几乎没有人能够真正享受连续坐几个小时的火车。

examples.en: Rarely do we see such a beautiful sunset.
examples.zh: 我们很少见到如此美丽的日落。

examples.en: Never have I seen such a magnificent building.
examples.zh: 我从未见过如此雄伟的建筑。

grammar.title: 形式主语it (Formal Subject It)
grammar.definition: 用it作形式主语，而把真正的主语（通常是不定式、动名词或从句）放在句子后面，使句子更加平衡。
grammar.structure: It + be + 形容词 + (for/of sb.) + to do sth.
grammar.usage: 当主语较长时，为了避免句子头重脚轻，使用形式主语it。

examples.en: It is almost impossible to take your mind off the journey.
examples.zh: 几乎不可能使自己不想到旅途。

examples.en: It is difficult to learn a new language.
examples.zh: 学习一门新语言是很难的。

examples.en: It is necessary for us to practice every day.
examples.zh: 我们每天练习是必要的。

grammar.title: 条件状语从句 (Conditional Clauses)
grammar.definition: 用于表达假设或条件的主从复合句，包括真实条件和虚拟条件。
grammar.structure: If + 主语 + 动词..., 主语 + 动词/情态动词...
grammar.usage: if引导的条件句可以表示真实条件或非真实（假设）的条件。

examples.en: If you are lucky enough to get a sleeper, you spend half the night staring at the small blue light.
examples.zh: 如果你足够幸运得到了卧铺，你会花半天时间盯着天花板上蓝色的小灯。

examples.en: If it is not, and you are likely to get seasick, no form of transport could be worse.
examples.zh: 如果海不平静，你可能会晕船，那么没有任何交通工具会比这更糟糕了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Hardly anyone can + 动词原形...

original.en: Hardly anyone can positively enjoy sitting in a train for more than a few hours.
original.zh: 几乎没有人能够真正享受连续坐几个小时的火车。

imitations.en: Hardly anyone can resist the temptation of free food.
imitations.zh: 几乎没有人能抵制免费食物的诱惑。

imitations.en: Hardly anyone can understand his complicated theory.
imitations.zh: 几乎没有人能理解他那套复杂的理论。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It is + 形容词 + to do sth.

original.en: It is almost impossible to take your mind off the journey.
original.zh: 几乎不可能使自己不想到旅途。

imitations.en: It is difficult to make a decision without all the facts.
imitations.zh: 没有全部事实很难做决定。

imitations.en: It is necessary to arrive early for the interview.
imitations.zh: 提前到达面试是必要的。

original.en: By comparison, ferry trips or cruises offer a great variety of civilized comforts.
original.zh: 相比之下，渡船旅行或游轮提供了多种多样的文明享受。

imitations.en: By comparison, living in the countryside is much quieter.
imitations.zh: 相比之下，住在乡下要安静得多。

imitations.en: By comparison, this restaurant offers better value for money.
imitations.zh: 相比之下，这家餐厅的性价比更高。

original.en: But nothing can match them for speed and comfort.
original.zh: 但就速度和舒适度而言，没有任何交通工具能比得上它们。

imitations.en: Nothing can replace the warmth of a mother's love.
imitations.zh: 没有什么能取代母爱的温暖。

imitations.en: Nothing can prevent him from reaching his goals.
imitations.zh: 没有什么能阻止他实现目标。
